nodes.name: ecc-install-config.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the user-authored install configuration contract — the input to ECC's installer specifying target harness, profile, modules, and include/exclude component selectors. Sits at the entry of the install pipeline as the requested intent before resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Draft-07 schema with enum-constrained targets and regex-pattern component selectors (baseline|lang|framework|capability:slug).

nodes.name: hooks.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the contract for Claude Code hooks configuration, validating event-keyed matcher entries and the three hook action types (command, http, prompt/agent). Describes runtime extension config the installer lays down for a target harness.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level oneOf permits either an object keyed by hook event names or a bare array of matcher entries; hook items dispatched via oneOf over $defs.

nodes.name: install-components.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the install components contract — the catalog of user-facing installable units (baseline, language, framework, capability, agent, skill, locale) where each component maps to one or more underlying modules. The most granular layer of the install selection pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Component id pattern enforces a family-prefixed slug; the family enum mirrors the id prefix set.

nodes.name: install-modules.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the install modules manifest — the mid-tier catalog of installable modules (rules, agents, commands, hooks, platform, skills, docs) carrying source paths, supported targets, dependencies, cost, and stability. Modules are the unit that components group and profiles select.
nodes.languageNotes: Strict additionalProperties:false with a fully-required property set; targets enum lists all supported harnesses.

nodes.name: install-profiles.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the install profiles contract — named presets (e.g. minimal, full) that each bundle a curated list of module ids. Profiles sit at the top of the install selection pipeline as coarse-grained, named entry points.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses propertyNames pattern + additionalProperties subschema to model a slug-keyed map of profile objects.

nodes.name: install-state.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the persisted install-state record (schemaVersion ecc.install.v1) capturing the outcome of an install: the resolved target, the original request, module resolution, source provenance, and the concrete file operations applied. The durable manifest written after applying an install config.
nodes.languageNotes: Records both the request (intent) and resolution (selected vs skipped modules) plus an operations log with strategy/ownership per file.

nodes.name: package-manager.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the package-manager preference contract — which JavaScript package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, bun) ECC uses and when the preference was last set. A small runtime configuration consulted by the install/build flow when fetching dependencies.

nodes.name: plugin.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the Claude plugin manifest contract — name, version, author, declared skills/commands/mcpServers, and a feature-count summary. Describes the plugin metadata the installer reads and lays down for a target harness.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses oneOf for polymorphic author (string|object) and mcpServers (string|array|object); semver-constrained version pattern.

nodes.name: provenance.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the provenance contract for learned and imported skills — origin source, creation timestamp, confidence score (0-1), and author. Required alongside skills in ~/.claude/skills/learned/* and imported/*, attesting where a skill came from.

nodes.name: state-store.schema.json
nodes.summary: Defines the ECC runtime state store (ecc.state-store.v1) — the central persistence contract holding sessions, skill runs and versions, decisions, embedded install state, governance events, and work items. The control-plane database backing ECC's continuous-learning and governance loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level arrays each $ref a $def; reusable scalar aliases (nonEmptyString, nullableString, jsonValue) factor out common field shapes across entity defs.

nodes.name: session
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a recorded ECC session: id, adapter/harness identity, lifecycle state, repo root, start/end timestamps, and a free-form snapshot blob.

nodes.name: skillRun
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a single skill execution: skill id/version, owning session, task description, outcome and failure reason, tokens/duration, and user feedback — the telemetry unit of the continuous-learning loop.

nodes.name: skillVersion
nodes.summary: Sub-schema tracking a versioned skill: skill id, version, content hash, amendment reason, and promotion/rollback timestamps — the version-control record for evolving skills.

nodes.name: decision
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a recorded decision (ADR-like): id, session, title, rationale, alternatives, supersedes link, status, and creation time — the governance record of architectural choices.

nodes.name: installState
nodes.summary: Sub-schema embedding install outcome inside the state store: target id/root, profile, resolved modules, file operations, install time, and source version — the in-store mirror of the standalone install-state manifest.

nodes.name: governanceEvent
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a governance event: id, session, event type, free-form payload, and resolution metadata — the audit/escalation record in ECC's governance loop.

nodes.name: workItem
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a tracked work item: id, source/sourceId, title, status, priority, url, owner, repo root, session link, and metadata — the external-task tracking record (issues/PRs) integrated into the store.

nodes.name: commandHookItem
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a command-type hook action: a local command (string or string array) with optional async flag and timeout.

nodes.name: httpHookItem
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for an http-type hook action: a target URL with optional headers, allowed environment variables, and timeout.

nodes.name: promptHookItem
nodes.summary: Sub-schema for a prompt/agent-type hook action: a prompt string with optional model override and timeout, dispatching an LLM-driven hook.

nodes.name: matcherEntry
nodes.summary: Sub-schema binding a matcher (string or object) to an array of hook items, with an optional description — the unit that maps a trigger condition to the hooks it fires.
